Meadow Buttercup (Ranunculus rapunculoides) is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help with wound healing, skin issues, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Meadow Buttercup include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Meadow Buttercup include teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions.
